assumptions: The question concerns foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements applying IFRS 9 hedge accounting.
The intragroup dividend gives rise to a recognised intercompany monetary receivable/payable once declared.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a fair value hedge as a hedge of changes in fair value of a recognised asset or liability, and the context includes foreign currency risk on qualifying intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a cash flow hedge as a hedge of variability in cash flows, and the context includes qualifying forecast intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify a net investment hedge as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk recognised in consolidated financial statements.
